element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Difficulty handicapping via city choice (wealthy cities like New York vs. resource-constrained cities like Lima)
Affects passenger volume, world event frequency, win condition thresholds (2.5M-4.5M passengers)
Core resource acquisition mechanic; foundation for all route expansion
Capital management; aircraft capability locked by geopolitical constraints (e.g., Moscow restricts to Soviet planes)
Continuous optimization: fare reduction, service quality, convenience improvements
Reactive decision-making; Cold War restrictions (lifted post-Perestroika) create temporal challenge escalation
Direct AI airline opposition; influences profitability on shared routes
Win condition tracking: profitability required; 4 consecutive negative quarters = bankruptcy
51% ownership threshold triggers assimilation; alternative growth path
Must connect all 22 cities + hit passenger threshold + maintain profitability simultaneously; final tension layer

typical_tension_triggers: World events announcement (labor strikes, political instability)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based quarterly cycles with asynchronous world event interruptions; tension builds through resource scarcity and competitive pressure; relief cycles on quarterly profit confirmation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Peripheral business opportunities (hotels, shuttles) are optional revenue sources; stock market acquisition path is optional alternative growth

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low pressure; headquarters and difficulty selection provide handicapping; early routes establish profitability baseline
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating competition; world events introduce unpredictability; geopolitical constraints compound resource decisions (Cold War era in Scenario 1)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multi-objective pressure: must link all 22 cities while maintaining profitability and hitting passenger threshold (2.5M-4.5M); approaching 32-year time limit creates resource urgency
notable_spikes: Cold War restrictions (Scenario 1, 1963-1989): Soviet airline paths blocked from Western negotiation and aircraft
Perestroika event: geopolitical landscape shift removes restrictions, creating new strategic window
Late-game network completion: exponential route negotiation complexity with 22-city requirement

notable_level_patterns.description: 1963-1995 timeframe emphasizes transpacific competition and Cold War geopolitical constraints. Player must balance Soviet vs. Western infrastructure, with increasing aircraft range unlocking intercontinental routes. Perestroika event provides mid-game difficulty shift.

1983-2015 timeframe focuses on faster aircraft technology adoption and modern competitive dynamics. Shorter timespan (32 years vs. 32 years) concentrates decision pressure; supersonic aircraft availability alters route viability.

Starting city choice creates asymmetric AI matchups: wealthy cities (New York, London, Tokyo) reduce handicap; poor cities (Lima, Nairobi, Honolulu) increase challenge. Same difficulty setting but inverted starting conditions.
